qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated SSRF via axiosRequestMake endpoint leaks internal service data
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes an unauthenticated SSRF via utils.controller.ts and utils.service.ts (axiosRequestMake endpoint), which is a different root cause (missing authentication guard, broken IP blocklist regex) and different vulnerable code from the advisory-described stored XSS via SVG upload with permissive MIME type classification in attachmentHelpers.ts and unsafe inline serving in attachments.controller.ts.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes stored XSS via SVG upload due to permissive MIME type classification in attachmentHelpers.ts and unsafe inline file serving in attachments.controller.ts. The only qualifying finding ('unauthenticated-ssrf-axios-request-make') describes unauthenticated SSRF via the axiosRequestMake endpoint in utils.controller.ts/utils.service.ts, which is a different vulnerability class, root cause, and code path from the advisory.
evidence: Advisory root cause: permissive MIME type check in attachmentHelpers.ts (image/svg+xml passes mimetype.includes('image')) and unsafe inline file serving in attachments.controller.ts (fileReadv3 endpoint).

Finding 'unauthenticated-ssrf-axios-request-make' root cause: missing authentication on axiosRequestMake endpoint (PublicApiLimiterGuard is a no-op) and broken IP blocklist regex in utils.service.ts - different endpoint, different code, different mechanism.
